module.name: fileio
module.spec: Session is the sole gateway for reads and writes to files under .drift/. Begin acquires an exclusive advisory lock on .drift/state.lock (creating the file if needed) using flock on Unix and LockFileEx on Windows; it blocks until the lock is acquired and auto-releases on process exit. Close releases the lock and is idempotent. There is no public Lock() method: callers must route all .drift/ I/O through a Session begun at the transaction boundary (typically the lifetime of a single CLI invocation). Concurrent Begin calls from different processes serialize; concurrent Begin calls within the same process block on each other (flock is not reentrant across separate file descriptors of the same file). See pinstore.load and pinstore.save for the transactional callers.

Session.Read(name) returns the bytes of .drift/<name>. A missing file returns an error for which os.IsNotExist reports true (callers use this to distinguish "fresh project" from "corrupt state"). Read does not take the lock per-call; it relies on the lock held since Begin. Read is safe for any file under .drift/ — state.xml, baselines.bin, user-settings.xml, theme.xml, .gitignore.

Session.Write(name, data) writes .drift/<name> atomically: it writes to a temp file in the same directory (so os.Rename never crosses a filesystem boundary) and then renames it over the target. POSIX rename is atomic, so a crash at any point leaves either the old content or the new content at the target path — never a partial write. Write creates .drift/ if it does not exist (MkdirAll on the directory before writing the temp file). The temp file is named <name>.tmp and is left in place if rename fails (caller can clean up). Write does not take the lock per-call; it relies on the lock held since Begin.
